memoryArtifacts.headline: Family of Herbert from Annals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777
memoryArtifacts.description: Family of Herbert from Annuals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777. However, it is noted, that Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) was not the Count of Vermandois, but rather his uncle was, Herbert IV, Count of Vermandois. It is also noted that the son of Herbertus Camerarius, Henry FitzHerbert (Henry the Treasurer) is missing from the Annals and Antiquities, but his grandson Herbert (Herbert FitzHenry) and great grandson Herbert  (Herbert FitzHerbert) are included.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Pedigree of the family of Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) in Collections Historical & Archaeological Relating to Montgomeryshire, Vol. V, pages 158-159
memoryArtifacts.description: Pedigree of the family of Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) in Collections Historical & Archaeological Relating to Montgomeryshire, Vol. V, pages 158-159

memoryArtifacts.headline: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius), in A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain and Ireland, Vol. 1, pg. 613 [but missing the generation of Henry the Treasurer)
memoryArtifacts.description: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius), in A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain and Ireland, Vol. 1, pg. 613 [but missing the generation of Henry the Treasurer). The record skips to the son of Henry, Herbert FitzHenry (Herbert FitzHerbert I, and his son Herbert FitzHerbert (II).

zh.lifeSummary: Hywel Ap Hywel於1314年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾。他於大約1337年與Margaret Cradock在威爾士結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1355年在威爾士布洛可爾聖 戴維斯享年41歲，埋葬在英國威爾斯布洛可爾聖 戴維斯。

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
meanings: A Welsh name. Some write the name Hoel, which they render 'lordly;' if so, it may come from huail, a vice-regent, regent. Arthur derives the surname Howell from Corn. -Brit, houl, the sun; and he adds, high, exalted.' But Hywel is rather from hywel, conspicuous.

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Hywell
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh name Hywel , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that name.

nameMeanings.name: Ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: Hywel Ap Hywel was born in 1314, in Monmouthshire, Wales. He married Margaret Cradock about 1337, in Wales. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1355, in St Davids, Pembrokeshire, Wales, at the age of 41, and was buried in St Davids, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Hywel Ap Hywelは1314年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアで生まれた。約1337年にウェールズでMargaret Cradockと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1355年にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアスト ダヴィズで41歳で死去し，イギリスウェールズペンブロケシュアイアスト ダヴィズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hywel Ap Hywel(은)는 1314년 웨일스 몬모우스실르에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1337년 웨일스에서 Margaret Cradock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1355년 웨일스 p브로케실르 스트 데이비드즈에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 웨일스 p브로케실르 스트 데이비드즈에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Hywel Ap Hywel родился в 1314 г. в местности монмоутшир, Уэльс. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Cradock около 1337 г. в местности Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1355 г. в местности ст дэвидс, пемброкешир, Уэльс в возрасте 41 год и был похоронен в местности ст дэвидс, пемброкешир, Уэльс, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Hywel Ap Hywel nació en 1314, en Monmouthshire, Gales. Se casó con Margaret Cradock aproximadamente 1337, en Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1355, en St Davids, Pembrokeshire, Gales, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultado en St Davids, Pembrokeshire, Gales, Reino Unido.
